name: Spicy Seafood Pasta
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Powdered chile pepper or hot Hungarian paprika
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
2. Dice the bacon into 1/4-inch squares and cook over medium-high heat until crispy. Remove from heat and set aside.
3. Add shallots to the pan with the cooked bacon and sauté until translucent.
4. Increase the heat to high, add shrimp, and cook until both sides are pink, about three to five minutes.
5. Add scallops and continue cooking until mostly done, stirring occasionally.
6. During this time, stir in the powdered chile pepper or hot Hungarian paprika.
7. Drain the cooked pasta and transfer it to a warmed serving bowl.
8. Pour the shellfish-bacon mixture over the pasta and add the heavy cream. Cook over high heat until the sauce reduces and thickens.
9. Stir in the sun-dried tomato paste while reducing the sauce further.
10. Once the sauce is thickened (around the same time the noodles are cooked), remove from heat.
11. Toss the pasta with the sauce, divide into serving bowls, and serve with grated Parmesan cheese.
